overview.name: spring-rest-sakila
overview.purpose: A Spring Boot REST API that exposes the classic Sakila DVD rental database (originally a MySQL sample schema) as a modern, hypermedia-driven web service. The application models a video rental business with domains for film catalog management, customer accounts, store operations, staff management, rentals, payments, and geographic locations. It provides CRUD operations, complex queries with filtering/pagination, sales reporting, and JWT-based authentication with role-based authorization.
overview.architecture_summary: Layered, domain-driven Spring Boot application organized into vertical service slices (auth, catalog, customer, location, payment, rental, staff, store). Each domain follows a consistent four-layer structure: REST controllers expose hypermedia endpoints, service interfaces define business contracts, repositories (Spring Data JPA + QueryDSL + Blaze-Persistence) handle persistence, and MapStruct mappers translate between JPA entities and DTOs. Cross-cutting concerns (security, logging, exception handling, serialization) are centralized in a common package. The architecture uses Spring HATEOAS RepresentationModelAssemblers to enrich responses with HAL links, QueryDSL for type-safe dynamic queries, and custom repository implementations for complex joins and projections.
frameworks: JTS (Java Topology Suite for spatial data)
key_components: com.example.app.common - Cross-cutting infrastructure: JWT security filters/providers, global exception handler, MDC logging filter, custom serializers, generic mapper contracts, utility classes for QueryDSL expression building
com.example.app.config - Spring configuration beans: security filter chain, Redis caching, JPA/QueryDSL/Blaze-Persistence factory setup, BCrypt password encoder, authentication manager
com.example.app.services.auth - JWT-based authentication: login controller, user details service (JPA-backed), authority entity with role enum (READ/WRITE/MANAGE/ADMIN)

com.example.app.services.catalog - Film/actor domain: CRUD for films and actors, film-actor associations, category/language/rating enums, full-text search entity (FilmText), custom queries for filtered listings and details
com.example.app.services.customer - Customer management: customer CRUD, embedded FullName value object, retrieval of customer payment/rental history with date filtering
com.example.app.services.location - Geographic data: address and city CRUD, country/language enums, JTS Point geometry converter for spatial coordinates
com.example.app.services.payment - Payment processing: payment CRUD, aggregated sales reports by category and store via custom QueryDSL joins
com.example.app.services.rental - Rental workflow: rent DVD (with stock check and payment creation), return DVD (with overdue calculation), rental status enum (RENT/RETURN)
com.example.app.services.staff - Staff management: staff CRUD, staff detail views with address/store joins
com.example.app.services.store - Store operations: store CRUD, inventory stock checks per film/store, staff assignment to stores, sales reporting facade
notable_patterns: Vertical slice architecture - each business domain is a self-contained package with its own controllers, services, repositories, entities, DTOs, mappers, and assemblers
HATEOAS/HAL - every REST response is enriched with hypermedia links via Spring HATEOAS RepresentationModelAssemblers; link relation names centralized in HalRelation constants
Custom repository pattern - Spring Data JPA repositories extend Custom*Repository interfaces with QueryDSL-based implementations (*RepositoryImpl) for complex queries returning DTOs
DTO-entity separation - MapStruct mappers handle bidirectional conversion; DTOs use nested static classes (e.g., ActorDto.Actor, ActorDto.ActorRequest, ActorDto.ActorResponse)
Enum-based domain constants - enums (Category, Country, Language, FilmRating, SpecialFeature, UserRole) with JPA AttributeConverters and static lookup maps for bidirectional conversion
Updatable contract - entities implement Updatable<T> interface to support in-place field updates from DTOs
JWT authentication filter chain - JwtAuthenticationFilter extracts Bearer token, JwtAuthenticationProvider validates and decodes JWT, JwtTokenProvider creates/signs tokens with HMAC-SHA
Redis caching - service-layer methods annotated with @Cacheable (e.g., FilmService.getFilm, ActorService.getActor) with 1-minute TTL
MDC logging - MdcLoggingFilter populates SLF4J MDC with X-Request-ID for request correlation
Standardized error responses - GlobalExceptionHandler converts exceptions to RFC 7807 ProblemDetail via ErrorResponseDto/ErrorCode enum
potential_risks: Empty custom repository implementations - Many Custom*RepositoryImpl classes are empty stubs (CustomCategoryRepositoryImpl, CustomFilmActorRepositoryImpl, CustomFilmCategoryRepositoryImpl, CustomFilmTextRepositoryImpl, CustomCityRepositoryImpl, CustomCountryRepositoryImpl, CustomLanguageRepositoryImpl, CustomInventoryRepositoryImpl) suggesting incomplete feature development or dead code

Dual country/language references in entities - CityEntity contains both a converted Country enum field AND a @ManyToOne CountryEntity, creating redundancy and potential consistency issues; similar pattern in FilmEntity with Language
Custom repository complexity - Heavy use of QueryDSL/Blaze-Persistence in custom repository implementations increases coupling to query frameworks and may hinder migration or testing
Stateless session + JWT - No token revocation mechanism evident; compromised tokens remain valid until expiry

N+1 query risk - Many entity relationships are lazily loaded (e.g., FilmEntity languages, ActorEntity associations) without explicit fetch strategies; custom repositories mitigate this for specific queries but standard repository methods may trigger multiple queries

Tight coupling between rental and other domains - RentalServiceImpl directly injects five other domain services (StoreService, PaymentService, FilmService, CustomerService, StaffService), creating cross-domain orchestration complexity
Enum ID stability - Several enums (Category, Country, Language) use hardcoded numeric IDs that must remain synchronized with database content; schema changes could break enum mappings
Missing validation in DTOs - Some DTOs lack @Valid constraints (e.g., InventoryDto, RentalDto variants) while others have comprehensive validation, creating inconsistent input validation

Role-based security granularity - Most endpoints secured with @Secured at method level but no field-level or data ownership checks; any authenticated user with correct role can access all records in that domain
Geometry converter fragility - GeometryConverter uses WKB byte serialization for JTS Point objects; database vendor changes or spatial type mismatches could cause runtime failures

insight.summary: Spring Boot application entry point for a REST API serving Sakila database content.

key_methods.name: main
key_methods.description: Entry point that bootstraps the Spring Boot application context and starts the embedded web server.

noteworthy: @SpringBootApplication annotation enables auto-configuration, component scanning, and configuration properties
Component scanning will start from the com.example.app package and scan all sub-packages
Standard Spring Boot main class with no customization or additional configuration

insight.summary: Defines a fixed enumeration of film categories (mirroring the classic Sakila DVD rental schema) each paired with a numeric id and display name, and exposes precomputed static maps for fast lookup by id, exact name, or lowercase name.
noteworthy: Uses Lombok @Getter and @RequiredArgsConstructor to generate accessors and the (id, category) constructor, reducing boilerplate.

Precomputes static unmodifiable lookup maps (ID_MAP, CATEGORY_MAP, CATEGORY_LOWER_MAP) at class-load time via Stream/Collectors for O(1) lookups by id, exact category name, or lowercased category name.
Nested static Constants class centralizes the string literals for category names, avoiding magic strings and enabling reuse in the enum constructor arguments.
Mirrors the 16 standard Sakila/DVD-rental style film categories plus a NONE/'-' sentinel value with id 0.

Static maps rely on Category::getId and Category::getCategory (Lombok-generated getters) being initialized before the map-building static initializers run, which works because enum constants are initialized before other static fields in declaration order.

insight.summary: Enumeration of 110 countries with associated IDs and display names, providing bidirectional lookup maps and a utility method for string manipulation.
insight.role: Enum constant definition with lookup utilities

key_methods.name: replaceCountryIdToCountryInString
key_methods.description: Finds the last numeric ID at the end of a string using regex, looks it up in ID_MAP, and replaces it with the corresponding country name.
key_methods.returns: String - the input string with the trailing numeric ID replaced by the country name

noteworthy: Two static unmodifiable maps (ID_MAP and COUNTRY_MAP) are built at class load time for O(1) lookup by ID or country name
Uses nested Constants class to define all country name strings, allowing reuse across the codebase
Lombok @Getter generates getId() and getCountry() accessors, and @RequiredArgsConstructor generates the enum constructor
Contains a NONE (id=0, name="-") placeholder entry, likely for null-object pattern usage
The replaceCountryIdToCountryInString method does not handle the case where the ID is not found in the map, which would cause a NullPointerException
Country list appears to be a legacy or partial set (includes Yugoslavia, excludes many modern countries)

insight.summary: Defines a standardized enumeration of application error codes with associated HTTP status codes, error levels, and descriptive phrases for consistent error handling across the application.
noteworthy: Uses Lombok @RequiredArgsConstructor to generate enum constructor and @Getter for field accessors
Each error code contains four attributes: a unique code string, an ErrorLevel, an HttpStatus for REST responses, and a human-readable phrase
Provides both ERROR and WARN level codes for different severity scenarios
Supports generic errors as well as domain-specific errors (e.g., RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND)
All enum constants are immutable with final fields

insight.summary: Defines an enumeration of error severity levels (INFO, WARN, ERROR), each paired with a numeric id and a human-readable name, used as a shared constant across the application for classifying error/log severity.
noteworthy: Uses Lombok @Getter and @RequiredArgsConstructor to generate accessors and constructor, avoiding boilerplate
Enum constants carry both a numeric id and a display name, likely used for serialization/logging/UI display purposes
No explicit lookup method (e.g., fromId) provided, so callers must iterate values() manually if needed

insight.summary: Enumeration representing film ratings (MPAA-style content ratings) with bidirectional mapping between enum constants and their string representations.
noteworthy: Uses an immutable static map (RATING_MAP) to enable reverse lookup from string rating to enum constant
Inner Constants class provides string literals that are reused in enum constructor calls, useful for consistent references across the codebase
Lombok @RequiredArgsConstructor generates constructor, @Getter generates getRating() accessor
Supports standard MPAA film ratings plus a NONE sentinel value represented as '-'

insight.summary: Defines a static catalog of field names representing HAL/HATEOAS link relation identifiers (e.g., self, create, film, customerList, storeSales) used across the application's domains. Leverages Lombok's @FieldNameConstants to generate compile-time String constants from these private field names rather than storing actual values, and is non-instantiable via a private constructor.
insight.role: constant definitions holder (HAL/HATEOAS link relation names via Lombok @FieldNameConstants)
noteworthy: Uses Lombok's @FieldNameConstants to auto-generate a nested 'Fields' class containing String constants for each field name, enabling type-safe references to these field names elsewhere (e.g., when building HATEOAS link relation names).
Private no-args constructor (@NoArgsConstructor(access = AccessLevel.PRIVATE)) prevents instantiation, indicating this class is meant purely as a compile-time constant holder, not a data object.
Fields themselves are never assigned or used for storing values; they exist solely so @FieldNameConstants can derive their names as constants.
Fields are grouped by domain/module via comments (others, catalog, customer, location, payment, rental, staff, store), mirroring the app's bounded contexts.
Likely used alongside Spring HATEOAS to standardize link relation name strings (e.g., HalRelation.Fields.self, HalRelation.Fields.filmList) across controllers/assemblers, avoiding magic strings.

insight.summary: Defines a fixed enumeration of supported languages, each paired with a numeric id and display name, and exposes static reverse-lookup maps (by id and by name) for efficient conversion between these representations.
insight.role: enum constant / lookup table (domain reference data)

key_methods.name: getId
key_methods.description: Lombok-generated accessor returning the numeric identifier of the language constant.

key_methods.name: getLanguage
key_methods.description: Lombok-generated accessor returning the human-readable language name.

noteworthy: Uses @Getter and @RequiredArgsConstructor from Lombok to avoid boilerplate constructor/getter code
Static ID_MAP and LANGUAGE_MAP built via Stream.collect(Collectors.toUnmodifiableMap(...)) at class-load time for O(1) reverse lookup by id or by name
Nested Constants inner class centralizes the string literals used both as enum field values and presumably referenced elsewhere for comparisons
Enum values encode a fixed domain vocabulary (e.g., mirrors a legacy 'language' reference table, likely from Sakila-like sample schema) mapped to fixed integer ids
Immutable maps (unmodifiableMap) protect against accidental mutation of the lookup tables
No validation/error handling if ID_MAP or LANGUAGE_MAP lookups miss (callers must handle null on get)

insight.summary: Enum defining special features available for films (trailers, commentaries, deleted scenes, behind the scenes) with bidirectional lookup capability via an immutable map.
noteworthy: Uses Lombok @Getter and @RequiredArgsConstructor to generate constructor and getter
FEATURE_MAP provides O(1) reverse lookup from String feature name to enum constant
FEATURE_MAP is immutable (Collectors.toUnmodifiableMap) to prevent runtime modification
Inner Constants class separates string literals from enum declaration for potential reuse
Typical usage: parsing user input or database values back to type-safe enum instances

insight.summary: Small DTO/factory helper that builds Spring's ProblemDetail (RFC 7807) error responses from an application-defined ErrorCode, standardizing HTTP status and detail message for error payloads.
insight.role: DTO / factory utility for error responses

key_methods.name: of
key_methods.description: Creates a ProblemDetail using the HTTP status from the given ErrorCode and sets its detail message to the ErrorCode's phrase; used to produce standardized error response bodies.
key_methods.returns: ProblemDetail - a populated ProblemDetail instance (not actually an instance of ErrorResponseDto itself)

noteworthy: Class extends ProblemDetail but the static factory method 'of' returns a plain ProblemDetail built via ProblemDetail.forStatus(), not an instance of ErrorResponseDto itself — the inheritance appears unused/misleading.
No instance fields or constructors are defined; the class currently only serves as a namespace for the static factory method.
Depends on an application-specific ErrorCode enum/class (com.example.app.common.constant.ErrorCode) providing getHttpStatus() and getPhrase().
Follows RFC 7807 Problem Details convention via Spring's ProblemDetail.

insight.summary: A simple Lombok-based DTO representing a person's full name, composed of firstName and lastName fields with size validation constraints, used for JSON serialization/deserialization and likely embedded within other DTOs.
noteworthy: Uses Lombok annotations (@Getter, @Setter, @ToString, @Builder, @NoArgsConstructor, @AllArgsConstructor) to generate boilerplate, keeping the class purely declarative
@FieldNameConstants generates a nested Fields class with constants for field names, useful for type-safe references (e.g., in queries or mapping)
Validation constraints (@Size min=1 max=45) enforce non-empty, bounded-length names at the bean validation layer
Jackson @JsonProperty annotations are redundant here since field names already match JSON property names, likely added for explicitness or documentation
Simple immutable-style DTO commonly nested within larger DTOs (e.g., customer or actor names) rather than used as a top-level API payload

insight.summary: A marker interface that defines a contract for DTOs to implement null-checking behavior, enabling classes to report whether all of their fields are null.

key_methods.name: areAllFieldsNull
key_methods.description: Contract method that implementing classes must override to check if all their fields are null. Returns true if all fields are null, false otherwise.
key_methods.returns: boolean - true if all fields are null

noteworthy: This is a simple behavioral contract interface with no implementation logic
Likely used in validation or filtering scenarios where empty/null DTOs need to be identified
Implementing classes bear the responsibility of defining what 'all fields null' means for their specific field set

insight.summary: Defines a generic contract for objects that can be updated in-place from another object of type E, commonly implemented by DTOs or entities to support partial/full state updates.

key_methods.name: update
key_methods.description: Applies the state of the given object to update the implementing instance, typically used to copy fields from a DTO onto an entity or vice versa.

noteworthy: Generic interface parameterized by type E, allowing type-safe update operations across different DTO/entity pairs.
Follows a simple update/merge pattern often used to sync entity state from an incoming DTO in service or mapper layers.
No default implementation; contract only, so behavior and null-handling semantics are left to implementers.

insight.summary: Embeddable JPA value object representing a person's full name, composed of firstName and lastName fields with validation and column mapping, intended to be embedded within owning entities.
noteworthy: Uses @Embeddable to be embedded as a composite value object within other entities rather than being a standalone entity.

Lombok @Builder, @Getter, @ToString, @NoArgsConstructor(PROTECTED), @AllArgsConstructor generate boilerplate; protected no-arg constructor enforces construction via builder or all-args constructor while satisfying JPA's requirement for a no-arg constructor.
Both fields are @NotNull and @Size(min=1, max=45), matching the @Column length=45 and nullable=false constraints for consistency between validation and schema.
No custom equals/hashCode defined despite being a value object embedded in entities, which could be relevant depending on how it's used in collections or comparisons.

insight.summary: A custom MapStruct qualifier annotation used to mark and identify specific mapping methods within mapper interfaces.
noteworthy: This is a meta-annotation that uses MapStruct's @Qualifier to create a custom qualifier
Retained at CLASS level (available to annotation processors like MapStruct at compile time but not at runtime)
Targets METHOD elements, meaning it can only be applied to methods
Used to disambiguate between multiple mapping methods when MapStruct needs to select a specific one for automatic mapping

insight.summary: Defines a MapStruct mapper contract for converting first/last name strings into a FullName domain entity, with the implementation auto-generated and wired as a Spring bean.

key_methods.name: mapToFullName
key_methods.description: Maps a pair of first and last name strings into a FullName domain object; MapStruct generates the implementation at compile time.
key_methods.returns: FullName - constructed full name domain entity

noteworthy: Interface only; implementation is generated by MapStruct annotation processor at build time
componentModel = "spring" registers the generated mapper as a Spring bean for dependency injection
No explicit mapping annotations (@Mapping) used, implying a simple constructor-based mapping convention in FullName

insight.summary: Defines a generic contract for bidirectional mapping between JPA/domain entities (T) and DTOs (U), including single-object and list/page conversions, to standardize mapper implementations across the application.
insight.role: Generic mapper interface (contract for entity-DTO conversion)

key_methods.name: mapToDto
key_methods.description: Converts a single entity instance into its corresponding DTO representation.

key_methods.name: mapToEntity
key_methods.description: Converts a single DTO instance back into its corresponding entity representation.

key_methods.name: mapToDtoList
key_methods.description: Converts a list of entities into a list of DTOs.

key_methods.name: mapToDtoList
key_methods.description: Converts a Spring Data Page of entities into a list of DTOs, typically used to map paginated query results for API responses.

key_methods.name: mapToEntityList
key_methods.description: Converts a list of DTOs back into a list of entities.

noteworthy: Generic interface parameterized by entity type T and DTO type U, intended to be implemented (likely via MapStruct) for each entity/DTO pair.
Overloaded mapToDtoList method distinguishes between List<T> and Page<T> input, allowing direct handling of paginated repository results without separate conversion logic.
No implementation provided here; concrete mappers implementing this interface would carry the actual mapping logic and any MapStruct annotations.

insight.summary: Custom Jackson StdSerializer that overrides default null serialization to output an empty string ("") instead of the JSON null literal, used to normalize null fields in API responses.

key_methods.name: serialize
key_methods.signature: public void serialize(Object value, JsonGenerator gen, SerializerProvider provider) throws IOException
key_methods.description: Writes an empty string token instead of a null value when serializing to JSON.

key_methods.name: serializeWithType
key_methods.signature: public void serializeWithType(Object value, JsonGenerator gen, SerializerProvider serializers, TypeSerializer typeSer) throws IOException
key_methods.description: Handles polymorphic/typed serialization case by also writing an empty string instead of null, ignoring the type serializer.

key_methods.name: getSchema
key_methods.signature: public JsonNode getSchema(SerializerProvider provider, Type typeHint)
key_methods.description: Provides a deprecated-style JSON schema node indicating the value is represented as an empty string.
key_methods.returns: JsonNode - schema node describing the 'empty_string' type

key_methods.name: acceptJsonFormatVisitor
key_methods.signature: public void acceptJsonFormatVisitor(JsonFormatVisitorWrapper visitor, JavaType typeHint) throws JsonMappingException
key_methods.description: Integrates with Jackson's JSON Schema module by declaring this serializer's output as a null format.

noteworthy: Registered typically via ObjectMapper.getSerializerProvider().setNullValueSerializer() or @JsonSerialize(nullsUsing=...) to globally/locally override default null handling.
acceptJsonFormatVisitor declares expectNullFormat while actual output is an empty string, a slight inconsistency between schema metadata and actual serialized value.
getSchema is part of the deprecated Jackson JSON Schema generation API (pre JSON Schema module).
Stateless singleton-friendly serializer; safe for reuse across threads.
Implements Serializable via StdSerializer with explicit serialVersionUID.

insight.summary: Centralized exception handler that intercepts application exceptions and converts them into standardized HTTP error responses with logging.

key_methods.name: handleResourceNotFoundException
key_methods.description: Handles ResourceNotFoundException by logging the error, printing stack trace, and returning a standardized error response with RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND error code.
key_methods.returns: ResponseEntity.HeadersBuilder<?> - HTTP response builder with RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND error details

key_methods.name: handleResourceNotAvailableException
key_methods.description: Handles ResourceNotAvailableException by logging the error, printing stack trace, and returning a standardized error response with RESOURCE_NOT_AVAILABLE error code.
key_methods.returns: ResponseEntity.HeadersBuilder<?> - HTTP response builder with RESOURCE_NOT_AVAILABLE error details

key_methods.name: handleException
key_methods.description: Catch-all handler for any unhandled Exception, logging the error with GENERIC_ERROR code and returning a standardized error response.
key_methods.returns: ResponseEntity.HeadersBuilder<?> - HTTP response builder with GENERIC_ERROR details

noteworthy: Extends ResponseEntityExceptionHandler to inherit default Spring exception handling behavior
All handlers call printStackTrace() which is generally discouraged in production as it's a blocking I/O operation and less structured than proper logging
Uses ResponseEntity.of() with ErrorResponseDto.of() pattern suggesting ErrorResponseDto returns an Optional
Return type ResponseEntity.HeadersBuilder<?> is unusual - typically handlers return ResponseEntity<?> directly
Three-tier exception handling: specific business exceptions (ResourceNotFoundException, ResourceNotAvailableException) and a generic fallback
Centralized error code mapping through ErrorCode enum provides consistency across error responses

insight.summary: Custom unchecked exception thrown when a requested resource is not available or accessible.
noteworthy: Extends RuntimeException, making it an unchecked exception that does not require explicit handling
Simple exception with only a message constructor; no additional fields or behavior
Part of the common exception hierarchy, likely used across multiple modules to signal resource unavailability

insight.summary: Custom unchecked exception thrown when a requested resource cannot be found in the system.
noteworthy: Extends RuntimeException, making it an unchecked exception that does not require explicit try-catch or throws declarations
Single-argument constructor accepts a message string that is passed to the parent RuntimeException
Commonly used in REST APIs or service layers to signal that an entity (e.g., user, product, order) was not found
Typically caught by a global exception handler to return appropriate HTTP status codes (e.g., 404 Not Found)

insight.summary: A servlet Filter that populates SLF4J's MDC with a request correlation ID (from the X-Request-ID header or a generated UUID) for the lifetime of each HTTP request, enabling request-scoped log correlation, and clears the MDC afterward to avoid context bleed between requests.

key_methods.name: doFilter
key_methods.signature: public void doFilter(ServletRequest request, ServletResponse response, FilterChain chain) throws IOException, ServletException
key_methods.description: Extracts or generates a request correlation ID and stores it in the SLF4J MDC under key 'request_id' for the duration of the request, then clears the MDC after the chain completes to prevent leakage across threads.

noteworthy: Reads 'X-Request-ID' header from incoming request; falls back to a randomly generated UUID if absent, enabling client-supplied trace correlation
Uses try/finally to guarantee MDC.clear() runs even if downstream filters/servlets throw, avoiding MDC context leakage in thread-pooled servlet containers
Does not propagate the generated/used request ID back to the response headers, so clients cannot see the correlation ID if one was auto-generated
Casts ServletRequest directly to HttpServletRequest without a instanceof check, which could throw ClassCastException in non-HTTP contexts (unlikely in typical web app deployments)

insight.summary: A Spring Security OncePerRequestFilter that intercepts incoming HTTP requests, extracts a Bearer JWT from the Authorization header, authenticates it via an AuthenticationManager, and populates the SecurityContext, while bypassing the /login endpoint.

key_methods.name: doFilterInternal
key_methods.signature: protected void doFilterInternal(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response, FilterChain filterChain) throws ServletException, IOException
key_methods.description: Extracts the Bearer token from the Authorization header, wraps it in a JwtAuthenticationToken, delegates authentication to the AuthenticationManager, and sets the resulting Authentication into the SecurityContextHolder before continuing the filter chain.

key_methods.name: shouldNotFilter
key_methods.description: Skips JWT authentication processing for requests to the /login servlet path.
key_methods.returns: boolean - true if the filter should be bypassed for the request

noteworthy: Extends OncePerRequestFilter to guarantee single execution per request within a single request dispatch.
Relies on a custom JwtAuthenticationToken (not shown) and an AuthenticationManager/AuthenticationProvider to perform actual token validation logic elsewhere.

No null/format validation beyond checking the 'Bearer ' prefix; malformed or invalid tokens will throw exceptions from authenticationManager.authenticate, which are not caught here and will propagate up (potentially requiring an exception handling filter/entry point elsewhere).
shouldNotFilter uses an exact string match on servlet path '/login', which is brittle if the login endpoint path changes or if using a context path.
Authentication result is stored in SecurityContextHolder using the default (ThreadLocal-based) strategy, so it must be cleared or handled appropriately for async requests.

insight.summary: Custom AuthenticationProvider that authenticates requests carrying a JwtAuthenticationToken by decoding the JWT access token, extracting the user's email and role-based authorities from token claims, and producing an authenticated JwtAuthenticationToken for use by Spring Security's authentication flow.
insight.role: Spring Security AuthenticationProvider implementation for JWT-based authentication

key_methods.name: authenticate
key_methods.signature: public Authentication authenticate(Authentication authentication) throws AuthenticationException
key_methods.description: Casts the incoming Authentication to JwtAuthenticationToken, decodes its access token via JwtTokenProvider, extracts email and authorities claims, maps authority strings to UserRole enum values implementing GrantedAuthority, and builds a fully authenticated JwtAuthenticationToken. Returns null if the token cannot be decoded.
key_methods.returns: Authentication - a populated JwtAuthenticationToken, or null if claims could not be decoded

key_methods.name: supports
key_methods.description: Indicates this provider only supports JwtAuthenticationToken instances.

noteworthy: Implements Spring Security's AuthenticationProvider SPI to plug custom JWT-based authentication into the AuthenticationManager chain.
Relies on unchecked/raw List type from claims.get('authorities', List.class), triggering unchecked cast warnings and risking ClassCastException if claim structure is malformed.
Assumes UserRole enum implements GrantedAuthority and that stored authority strings exactly match enum constant names, otherwise UserRole.valueOf throws IllegalArgumentException.

Returning null from authenticate() on failed decode is unusual for Spring Security providers; typically an AuthenticationException should be thrown instead, which could lead to ambiguous authentication failures downstream.
No null-check on authorities list before iterating, risking NullPointerException if claim is absent.
Delegates actual token parsing/validation to JwtTokenProvider, keeping this class focused solely on translating claims into a Spring Security Authentication object.

insight.summary: Custom Spring Security authentication token that represents a JWT-based authentication, storing the access token and user email as principal.

key_methods.name: JwtAuthenticationToken
key_methods.signature: public JwtAuthenticationToken(String accessToken, String email, Collection<? extends GrantedAuthority> authorities)
key_methods.description: Constructs an authenticated token with the JWT access token, user email, and granted authorities. Marks the token as authenticated immediately.

key_methods.name: JwtAuthenticationToken
key_methods.description: Constructs an unauthenticated token with only the access token, used for tokens that have not yet been validated. No authorities or email are set.

key_methods.name: getCredentials
key_methods.description: Returns the JWT access token as the credentials for this authentication.

key_methods.name: getPrincipal
key_methods.description: Returns the user's email address as the principal for this authentication.

noteworthy: Extends AbstractAuthenticationToken to integrate with Spring Security's authentication framework
Two constructors serve different purposes: one for authenticated tokens (with email and authorities) and one for unauthenticated tokens (token-only)
The authenticated constructor immediately calls setAuthenticated(true), which should only be done after validation
Uses Lombok @Getter to generate accessors for accessToken and email fields
The single-parameter constructor takes a parameter named 'substring' which is misleading - it should represent a full token

insight.summary: Spring-managed component responsible for creating and decoding JWT tokens used for authentication, signing tokens with an HMAC-SHA key derived from a configured secret and embedding user email/authorities as claims.
insight.role: Security utility / JWT token provider component

key_methods.name: init
key_methods.description: Initializes the HMAC-SHA signing key from the configured signing-key property after bean construction, using PostConstruct lifecycle callback.

key_methods.name: createToken
key_methods.description: Builds a signed JWT containing the user's email and authorities as claims, with an issued-at timestamp set to the current time in Asia/Seoul timezone.

key_methods.name: decodeToken
key_methods.description: Parses and validates a JWT string using the signing key, returning the claims body if the signature is valid.
key_methods.returns: Claims - decoded claims body of the JWT

noteworthy: Signing key is injected via @Value from app.jwt.signing-key property and converted to a SecretKey using Keys.hmacShaKeyFor, requiring sufficient key length for the HMAC algorithm chosen by jjwt.
No explicit token expiration (setExpiration) is set on created tokens, which may be a security concern (tokens effectively never expire).
Hardcodes 'Asia/Seoul' timezone for issuedAt, which could be a portability/consistency concern if the app is deployed across timezones.
decodeToken will throw jjwt exceptions (e.g., ExpiredJwtException, SignatureException, MalformedJwtException) on invalid tokens; no exception handling is done here, so callers must handle these.
Authorities are stored as a raw list of GrantedAuthority objects in claims, which may not serialize/deserialize cleanly depending on JSON serializer used by jjwt.
Uses @RequiredArgsConstructor from Lombok but has no final fields, making it functionally a no-op annotation here.

insight.summary: Stateless, non-instantiable utility class providing null-safe helper methods to conditionally build QueryDSL BooleanExpression predicates (equality and range/between filters) for use in dynamic, criteria-style database queries, avoiding manual null checks when composing filters.
insight.role: Utility class for dynamic QueryDSL predicate construction

key_methods.name: filterExpression
key_methods.signature: public static BooleanExpression filterExpression(boolean condition, BooleanExpression expression)
key_methods.description: Returns the given expression only if the condition is true, otherwise null, enabling conditional QueryDSL predicate composition without null checks scattered across query-building code.

key_methods.name: equalExpression
key_methods.signature: private static <T extends Comparable<?>, U extends ComparableExpressionBase<T>> BooleanExpression equalExpression(U path, T value)

noteworthy: Final utility class with private constructor via Lombok @NoArgsConstructor(access = AccessLevel.PRIVATE), preventing instantiation.
Relies on returning null BooleanExpressions, which QueryDSL's BooleanBuilder/where() treats as 'no-op' predicates for conditional dynamic query building.

Generic bounded type parameters (T extends Comparable<?>, U extends ComparableExpressionBase<T>) are used to support multiple QueryDSL path/expression types with a single private implementation, reducing duplication.
No null-check on the 'path' argument itself; assumes callers always pass a valid QueryDSL path/expression.

Overload resolution relies on distinct QueryDSL types (NumberExpression vs NumberPath, EnumExpression vs EnumPath, DateTimeExpression vs DateTimePath) rather than a common supertype for the public API, likely due to API surface differences between QueryDSL's Expression and Path hierarchies.

key_methods.description: Maps an InventoryEntity to its DTO representation, explicitly ignoring the filmByFilmId and storeByStoreId associations to avoid deep/circular mapping of related entities.

noteworthy: Extends a shared GenericMapper<E, D> interface, standardizing mapping contracts across the codebase
Uses MapStruct's componentModel = "spring" to generate a Spring-managed bean implementation at build time
Explicitly ignores filmByFilmId and storeByStoreId mappings, likely to prevent lazy-loading issues or circular references when converting nested entity relations
No custom mapToEntity override present, so default GenericMapper behavior is used for entity conversion
Interface has no method body; actual implementation is generated by the MapStruct annotation processor

insight.summary: MapStruct mapper interface responsible for converting between StoreEntity and Store DTOs (StoreDto.Store, StoreDto.StoreRequest). Extends a generic mapper for standard entity<->DTO conversion and adds a custom mapping method for creating entities from request DTOs, ignoring relational/generated fields and auto-setting lastUpdate.

key_methods.name: mapToEntity
key_methods.description: Maps a StoreRequest DTO to a new StoreEntity, ignoring the storeId (auto-generated), staffByManagerStaffId and addressByAddressId (relational fields set elsewhere), and setting lastUpdate to the current timestamp via an inline expression.

noteworthy: Uses componentModel = "spring" to register as a Spring bean
Uses imports attribute to bring LocalDateTime into scope for the expression
lastUpdate is set via a Java expression (LocalDateTime.now()) rather than mapped from the DTO, meaning it always reflects mapping time not request time
Relational fields (staffByManagerStaffId, addressByAddressId) are intentionally ignored here, implying they must be set/resolved separately (likely in a service layer) before persistence
Inherits standard bidirectional mapping methods (e.g., toDto/toEntity) from GenericMapper<StoreEntity, StoreDto.Store>, not redefined in this file

insight.summary: Spring Data JPA repository interface for querying and persisting InventoryEntity records, with custom query methods for retrieving inventory by film and store.

key_methods.name: findAllByFilmId
key_methods.description: Retrieves all inventory records associated with a specific film ID using Spring Data JPA derived query method.
key_methods.returns: List<InventoryEntity> - all inventory entries for the given film

key_methods.name: findAllByStoreIdAndFilmId
key_methods.description: Retrieves all inventory records for a specific film at a specific store using Spring Data JPA derived query method.
key_methods.returns: List<InventoryEntity> - all inventory entries matching both store and film

noteworthy: Extends both JpaRepository (for CRUD operations) and CustomInventoryRepository (for custom query logic)
Uses Spring Data JPA derived query methods that are automatically implemented based on method naming conventions
Entity primary key type is Integer
Marked with @Repository annotation for component scanning and exception translation

insight.summary: Spring Data JPA repository interface for StoreEntity, providing standard CRUD operations via JpaRepository plus custom query methods defined in CustomStoreRepository.
noteworthy: Annotated with @Repository for Spring component scanning/exception translation.
Extends JpaRepository<StoreEntity, Integer> for standard CRUD and paging/sorting support.
Extends CustomStoreRepository to incorporate custom query implementations, following the Spring Data custom repository fragment pattern.
No additional methods declared; all behavior is inherited from the extended interfaces.

insight.summary: An empty custom repository interface intended to declare additional, non-standard data access methods for the Inventory entity beyond what Spring Data JPA's generated repositories provide.
insight.role: Repository interface (custom Spring Data JPA repository fragment)
noteworthy: Currently an empty marker interface with no declared methods; likely a placeholder intended to be combined with a base Spring Data repository via the custom repository fragment pattern.
No implementation class (e.g. CustomInventoryRepositoryImpl) is present in this snippet, so this interface has no effect until methods and an implementation are added.
Follows Spring Data JPA convention where a 'CustomXxxRepository' interface is extended by 'XxxRepository' and backed by 'CustomXxxRepositoryImpl' to add custom query logic beyond standard CRUD.

insight.summary: Empty/stub implementation of the CustomInventoryRepository interface intended to house custom query logic for inventory persistence beyond standard Spring Data repository methods; currently contains no fields or methods.
insight.role: Custom repository implementation (Spring Data custom repository fragment)
noteworthy: Empty implementation: implements CustomInventoryRepository but defines no methods or fields, suggesting incomplete implementation or a stub
@RequiredArgsConstructor has no final fields to inject, making it currently a no-op annotation
Custom repository pattern used to extend Spring Data JPA repositories with custom query logic, but no logic has been added yet
@Repository marks this as a Spring-managed bean for exception translation and component scanning

insight.summary: Defines a custom repository contract for retrieving store-related details and associated staff data as DTOs, intended to be combined with a Spring Data JPA repository via the custom repository pattern.

key_methods.name: findAllStoreDetailsList
key_methods.description: Retrieves a list of detailed store information, likely via a custom query joining store-related entities.

key_methods.name: findStoreDetailsById
key_methods.description: Fetches detailed information for a single store by its identifier, returning empty if not found.

key_methods.name: findAllStoreStaffList
key_methods.description: Retrieves the list of staff members associated with a given store.

key_methods.name: findStoreStaffById
key_methods.description: Fetches a single staff member belonging to a specific store by staff identifier, returning empty if not found.

noteworthy: Interface only defines contract; implementation (likely QueryDSL or JPQL-based) is expected elsewhere, typically named CustomStoreRepositoryImpl
Returns DTOs directly rather than entities, suggesting projection queries that join store, address, staff, and related tables
Uses Optional for single-result queries to avoid null returns
Part of the custom repository pattern used to extend Spring Data JPA repositories with complex/custom queries

insight.summary: Custom JPA repository implementation for complex store queries using QueryDSL. Provides methods to fetch store details with aggregated address and manager information, and to retrieve staff lists for specific stores. Handles country ID to country name replacement in result strings.

key_methods.name: findAllStoreDetailsList
key_methods.description: Fetches all stores with their details (city, manager name, full address). Post-processes results to replace country IDs with country names.
key_methods.returns: List<StoreDetailsDto.StoreDetails> - all store details with address and manager info

key_methods.name: findStoreDetailsById
key_methods.description: Fetches a single store's details by ID, including aggregated address and manager information. Replaces country IDs with country names before returning.
key_methods.returns: Optional<StoreDetailsDto.StoreDetails> - store details for the given ID

key_methods.name: findStoreDetail
key_methods.description: Builds a QueryDSL query that joins store, staff, address, and city entities to construct StoreDetails DTOs with concatenated strings for store location and full address. Conditionally filters by store ID if provided.
key_methods.returns: JPAQuery<StoreDetailsDto.StoreDetails> - query builder for store details

key_methods.name: findAllStoreStaffList
key_methods.description: Retrieves all staff members associated with a specific store ID.
key_methods.returns: List<StaffDto.Staff> - all staff members for the given store

key_methods.name: findStoreStaffById
key_methods.signature: public Optional<StaffDto.Staff> findStoreStaffById(Integer storeId, Integer staffId)
key_methods.description: Retrieves a specific staff member by staff ID within a given store ID.
key_methods.returns: Optional<StaffDto.Staff> - specific staff member in the given store

key_methods.name: findStoreStaff
key_methods.signature: private JPAQuery<StaffDto.Staff> findStoreStaff(Integer storeId, Integer staffId)
key_methods.description: Builds a QueryDSL query to fetch staff members filtered by store ID, with optional additional filtering by staff ID.
key_methods.returns: JPAQuery<StaffDto.Staff> - query builder for staff members

key_methods.name: replaceCountryIdToCountry
key_methods.description: Post-processes store details by replacing country ID placeholders in store and address strings with actual country names using a utility method.
key_methods.returns: void - modifies the input DTO in place

noteworthy: Uses QueryDSL Q-types for type-safe query construction (QStoreEntity, QStaffEntity, QAddressEntity, QCityEntity)
Leverages Projections.constructor to map query results directly to DTO constructors
String concatenation is performed at the database level using QueryDSL Expressions for building composite address strings
Country ID replacement is a post-processing step applied after fetching results, suggesting country IDs are stored as integers but need to be displayed as names
Optional.of is used without null checks in findStoreDetailsById and findStoreStaffById, which could throw NPE if fetchFirst() returns null
Repository pattern with custom implementation suffix (Impl) following Spring Data JPA conventions
Constructor injection via Lombok @RequiredArgsConstructor for JPAQueryFactory dependency

insight.summary: Defines the contract for generating sales reports, specifically aggregated sales data by category and by store, to be implemented by a concrete service class.

key_methods.name: reportSalesByCategory
key_methods.description: Generates a sales report aggregated by product category.
key_methods.returns: List<CategorySalesDto.CategorySales> - list of category sales aggregates

key_methods.name: reportSalesByStore
key_methods.description: Generates a sales report aggregated by store.
key_methods.returns: List<StoreSalesDto.StoreSales> - list of store sales aggregates

noteworthy: Pure interface with no implementation, defining a contract for reporting/analytics services
Uses nested DTO static classes (CategorySalesDto.CategorySales, StoreSalesDto.StoreSales) to represent aggregated report data
No pagination or filtering parameters exposed, suggesting reports return full aggregated result sets

insight.summary: Service implementation that generates sales reports by aggregating payment data across categories and stores.

key_methods.name: reportSalesByCategory
key_methods.description: Generates a sales report grouped by category by delegating to the payment repository's aggregation query.
key_methods.returns: List<CategorySalesDto.CategorySales> - aggregated sales data grouped by category

key_methods.name: reportSalesByStore
key_methods.description: Generates a sales report grouped by store by delegating to the payment repository's aggregation query.
key_methods.returns: List<StoreSalesDto.StoreSales> - aggregated sales data grouped by store

noteworthy: Implements the ReportService interface
All report methods are annotated with @Transactional(readOnly = true) for optimized read-only database access
Thin service layer - business logic is delegated to repository-level aggregation queries
Uses constructor injection via Lombok's @RequiredArgsConstructor for PaymentRepository dependency
Despite being in the 'store' service package, it depends on PaymentRepository from the 'payment' service package, indicating cross-service dependency

noteworthy: Implements StoreService interface, following a service-layer pattern with constructor injection via Lombok's @RequiredArgsConstructor.
Uses @Transactional(readOnly = true) for query methods and @Transactional for mutating methods, a good practice for JPA transaction boundaries.

Several methods (addStoreStaff, updateStoreStaff, removeStoreStaff) are stubbed/unimplemented (return null or no-op), indicating incomplete feature implementation - potential bug or TODO for reviewers.
getStore returns Optional.of() wrapping a value already guaranteed non-null since orElseThrow is used, making the Optional return type somewhat redundant/misleading.
Relies on custom repository query methods (findAllStoreDetailsList, findStoreDetailsById, findAllStoreStaffList, findStoreStaffById) suggesting projection-based queries in StoreRepository.
Uses mapper classes (StoreMapper, InventoryMapper) to convert between entities and DTOs, keeping persistence models decoupled from API-facing DTOs.
Entity mutation via entity.update(...) in updateStore suggests a domain-driven design style where entities encapsulate their own update logic.
Throws ResourceNotFoundException consistently for missing resources, supporting centralized exception handling likely via a @ControllerAdvice.
